frameworks.name: grouping-and-segregation
frameworks.one_line: structural inference over similarity and distinct identity supplements sensory samples in memory.

frameworks.name: active inference
frameworks.one_line: retrieval infers feature values from sensory samples plus structural memory.

frameworks.name: repulsive bias account
frameworks.one_line: reported repulsion in working-memory recall as one bias regime.

frameworks.name: attractive bias account
frameworks.one_line: reported attraction in working-memory recall as the opposing regime.

core_question: Can a single normative framework reconcile both repulsive and attractive biases in visual working memory recall?
key_assumptions: memory stores noisy sensory samples plus discrete structural information
retrieval performs active inference combining sensory and structural information
grouping and segregation are fundamental memory operations
manipulations: angular distance between two dots varied from 6 to 60 degrees
key_findings: recall biases depended systematically on inter-item distance
framework predicts the bias pattern and observed variance and correlation structure
interpretations: grouping and segregation jointly yield efficient, robust memory representations
repulsive and attractive recall biases arise from a single structural-inference mechanism
